name: QuickNode API
what_it_does: Managed blockchain node infrastructure providing JSON-RPC and REST endpoints for Ethereum, Solana, and 40+ other chains, eliminating the need to run self-hosted nodes.
use_cases: Agents interacting with smart contracts on Ethereum or other EVM chains via eth_call
DeFi protocol agents querying on-chain state — balances, pool reserves, prices
NFT agents reading token metadata, ownership, and transfer history
Transaction submission agents broadcasting signed transactions to the network
Event monitoring agents subscribing to smart contract logs via WebSocket
not_for: Centralized exchange trading — QuickNode is for on-chain interactions, not exchange APIs
Teams that only need one chain and are comfortable with Infura or Alchemy's simpler pricing
best_when: An agent needs reliable, low-latency access to multiple blockchain networks without operational overhead, especially for multi-chain DeFi or NFT workflows.
avoid_when: You only need Ethereum mainnet and want the simplest possible setup — Infura's free tier may be sufficient for basic use cases.

auth.notes: API key is embedded in the endpoint URL (e.g., https://your-endpoint.quiknode.pro/YOUR_API_KEY/). No separate header needed — the key is part of the endpoint slug. This means the key is visible in code that stores the URL. Endpoint-level access control available via dashboard.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 10 million API credits/month on Free plan; limited to a single chain; no add-ons
pricing.paid_tiers: Build $49/month (30M credits, multi-chain); Scale $299/month (100M credits, priority support); Custom plans above that
pricing.notes: Credit-based pricing like CoinMarketCap — different RPC methods consume different credit amounts. Free tier is generous for development but production DeFi agents may exhaust 10M credits quickly. Multi-chain access requires paid plan.

performance.rate_limits: Free: up to 15 requests/second; Build: 25 req/s; Scale: 50 req/s; credits are the primary constraint at higher throughput

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON-RPC errors follow the standard error object format with code and message fields. Standard codes (-32600, -32601, -32602, etc.) are used consistently. Some chain-specific errors may have non-obvious messages. Generally parseable by any JSON-RPC client.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read calls (eth_call, eth_getBalance, etc.) are idempotent by definition. Transaction submission (eth_sendRawTransaction) is not idempotent — duplicate submissions will fail with 'already known' or 'nonce too low' errors, which agents can detect and handle.
known_agent_gotchas: API key is embedded in the endpoint URL — ensure the URL is treated as a secret and not logged or committed to source control
Credit consumption varies by RPC method — eth_getLogs on large block ranges can consume thousands of credits in a single call; always specify block ranges narrowly
Multi-chain requires separate endpoints per chain — agents managing multiple chains need to maintain multiple endpoint URLs
WebSocket subscriptions (eth_subscribe) are not available on all plans and chains — check compatibility before building real-time event agents
Ethereum archive data (historical state before the pruning window) requires an archive node add-on, which costs extra
Free plan is limited to a single chain — testing multi-chain behavior requires upgrading or using multiple free accounts

security_score_components.security_notes: Embedding the API key in the endpoint URL is a security anti-pattern — the key appears in HTTP logs, browser history, and any place the URL is stored. No per-method scopes. TLS enforced. QuickNode allows IP allowlisting and domain restrictions on paid plans.
